ddls:
CREATE TABLE "job_postings" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job posting */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the job posting */,
  "salary_min" REAL /* Minimum salary range for the job posting */,
  "salary_max" REAL /* Maximum salary range for the job posting */,
  "details" TEXT /* Detailed description of the job posting */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the job posting was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the job posting was last updated */,
  "industry_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the industry the job posting belongs to */,
  "company_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the company that created the job posting */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location of the job posting */,
  "job_category" TEXT /* Category of the job (e.g., Full-time, Part-time, Internship) */,
  "employment_type" TEXT /* Type of employment (e.g., Permanent, Contract) */,
  "experience_required" TEXT /* Experience required for the job (e.g., 2+ years, Entry-level) */,
  "benefits" TEXT /* Benefits offered (e.g., Health insurance, Paid time off) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_postings_company_id FOREIGN KEY ("company_id") REFERENCES companies ("company_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_postings_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_postings_industry_id FOREIGN KEY ("industry_id") REFERENCES industries ("industry_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "industries" (
  "industry_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each industry */,
  "industry_name" TEXT /* Name of the industry */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the industry */,
  "parent_industry_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the parent industry (for hierarchical industry categories) */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the industry is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("industry_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "companies" (
  "company_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each company */,
  "company_name" TEXT /* Name of the company */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the company */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the company */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the company */,
  "website" TEXT /* Company's website */,
  "address" TEXT /* Company's address */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the company's account is active */,
  "company_size" TEXT /* Size of the company (e.g., Small, Medium, Large) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("company_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the location */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the location */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the location is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_seekers" (
  "seeker_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job seeker */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the job seeker */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the job seeker */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the job seeker */,
  "resume" TEXT /* Resume of the job seeker */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the job seeker */,
  "current_company" TEXT /* Current company of the job seeker */,
  "current_title" TEXT /* Current job title of the job seeker */,
  "linkedin_profile" TEXT /* LinkedIn profile URL */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the job seeker's account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("seeker_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "resumes" (
  "resume_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each resume */,
  "seeker_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the job seeker who uploaded the resume */,
  "file_name" TEXT /* File name of the resume */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the resume in MB */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date and time the resume was uploaded */,
  "file_type" TEXT /* Type of the resume file (e.g., PDF, DOCX) */,
  "is_public" INTEGER /* Indicates if the resume is public or private */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("resume_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_resumes_seeker_id FOREIGN KEY ("seeker_id") REFERENCES job_seekers ("seeker_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_applications" (
  "application_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job application */,
  "job_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the job posting being applied for */,
  "seeker_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the job seeker who made the application */,
  "application_date" TEXT /* Date and time the application was made */,
  "application_status" TEXT /* Status of the application (e.g., Pending, Interview, Rejected) */,
  "interview_scheduled_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the scheduled interview */,
  "interview_notes" TEXT /* Notes from the interview */,
  "hiring_manager_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the hiring manager */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("application_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_applications_job_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_id") REFERENCES job_postings ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_applications_seeker_id FOREIGN KEY ("seeker_id") REFERENCES job_seekers ("seeker_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_applications_hiring_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("hiring_manager_id") REFERENCES companies ("company_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "industry_average_salaries" (
  "industry_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the industry */,
  "average_salary" REAL /* Average salary range for the industry */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the average salary was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("industry_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "location_average_salaries" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location */,
  "average_salary" REAL /* Average salary range for the location */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the average salary was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_posting_tags" (
  "posting_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the job posting */,
  "tag" TEXT /* Tag for the job posting (e.g., software engineer, data scientist) */,
  "tag_type" TEXT /* Type of tag (e.g., Skill, Responsibility) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("posting_id", "tag")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_seeker_skills" (
  "seeker_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the job seeker */,
  "skill" TEXT /* Skill of the job seeker (e.g., programming languages, data analysis) */,
  "proficiency_level" TEXT /* Proficiency level of the skill (e.g., Beginner, Intermediate, Expert) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("seeker_id", "skill")
)

CREATE TABLE "education" (
  "education_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each education record */,
  "seeker_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the job seeker */,
  "degree" TEXT /* Degree earned by the job seeker */,
  "institution" TEXT /* Institution where the degree was earned */,
  "graduation_date" TEXT /* Graduation date */,
  "major" TEXT /* Major of the degree */,
  "gpa" REAL /* Grade Point Average */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("education_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_education_seeker_id FOREIGN KEY ("seeker_id") REFERENCES job_seekers ("seeker_id")
)
